label: Autoencoders

node.label: Autoencoders
node.definition: An autoencoder compresses an input into a latent code and reconstructs it, learning representations through an information bottleneck rather than explicit labels.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Autoencoders is modeled as depending on Affine transforms & dense layers, Reconstruction objectives and feeding downstream nodes such as Variational autoencoders, VQ-VAE & learned discrete tokenizers, Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis.
examples: atlas downstream link to Variational autoencoders
atlas downstream link to VQ-VAE & learned discrete tokenizers
atlas downstream link to Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Affine transforms & dense layers, Reconstruction objectives.

dependencies.rationale: Autoencoders is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.

In this atlas graph, Autoencoders has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Reconstruction objectives because Reconstruction objectives is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
Autoencoders consumes, emits, or organizes information using Latent variables & hidden state.
